subject: Economics
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the interdependency of the four factors of production
Evaluate the transition from traditional to modern farming methods
Assess the socio-economic impact of the Green Revolution
Examine the challenges of land sustainability and groundwater depletion
Understand the credit cycle and debt traps in rural economies
Investigate the growth potential of non-farm sectors

lesson_plan.title: Organization of Production & Capital Theory
topics_covered: Detailed study of the four requirements of production
Deep dive into Physical Capital: Fixed vs. Working
The role of Human Capital in coordinating resources
teaching_strategy: Case study analysis of a small business
Comparative charting of different capital types
student_activities: Create a 'Production Plan' for a hypothetical local business
Categorize assets into Fixed and Working capital with justification
assessment: Short analytical paragraph on the importance of Human Capital

lesson_plan.title: Intensive Farming & Multiple Cropping
topics_covered: Techniques to increase yield from fixed land
The role of electricity in transforming irrigation
Multiple cropping vs. modern farming
teaching_strategy: Discussion on the efficiency of electric tubewells
student_activities: Calculate crop intensity for a 2-hectare plot
Map the change in Palampur's irrigation over decades

lesson_plan.title: The Green Revolution: Success and Costs
topics_covered: Introduction of HYV seeds and chemical inputs
Regional disparities in agricultural growth
Initial surge in productivity
teaching_strategy: Data analysis of wheat and rice production (1965-present)
Comparative study of Punjab vs. other states
student_activities: Interpret a bar graph showing the rise in food grain production
Identify the specific inputs required for an HYV 'package'
assessment: Oral questioning on Green Revolution mechanics

lesson_plan.title: Land Sustainability & Environmental Ethics
topics_covered: Scientific reports on soil fertility loss
Groundwater table depletion
The trade-off between current yield and future resources
teaching_strategy: Socratic questioning on 'Will the land sustain?'
Presentation on chemical fertilizer impact on soil microorganisms
student_activities: Write a 'Letter to the Editor' regarding falling water tables
Group discussion on sustainable farming alternatives
assessment: Evaluation of the 'Sustainability Report' written by students

lesson_plan.title: Social Structure and Land Distribution
topics_covered: Inequality in land ownership (450 families case study)
The plight of landless laborers and small farmers
Wages and work conditions in rural markets
teaching_strategy: Using pie charts to visualize inequality
Discussion on why wages are lower than government minimums
student_activities: Construct a pie chart based on Palampur's land distribution data
Role-play a wage negotiation between a laborer and a large farmer
assessment: Analytical note on rural labor competition

lesson_plan.title: Rural Credit and the Debt Trap
lesson_plan.objective: Understand how capital is arranged and the risks involved
topics_covered: Sources of capital for small vs. large farmers
Interest rates and the cycle of debt
The concept of 'Marketable Surplus'
teaching_strategy: Mathematical comparison of moneylender vs. bank interest
Flowcharting the profit reinvestment of large farmers
student_activities: Calculate the total repayment for a small farmer's loan
Analyze the 'Story of Savita' to identify her economic barriers

lesson_plan.title: Diversification & Non-Farm Economy
topics_covered: Dairy, Small manufacturing, and Trade
The role of transport and IT in village growth
Barriers to starting non-farm activities
teaching_strategy: Summary mind-mapping of the entire chapter
student_activities: Draft a mini-business plan for a village-based non-farm activity
Participate in a whole-chapter revision quiz

teaching_aids: Data sheets on Indian agricultural production
Case study handouts (Savita and Tejpal Singh)
teacher_tip: Focus on the 'vicious cycle' of debt for small farmers versus the 'virtuous cycle' of reinvestment for large farmers to help students grasp systemic inequality.
